what_it_does: Records an open noticeboard issue that presents channel-log evidence to refute the claim that ChatGPT was repeating messages, diagnosing the repeats as a channel-delivery replay/re-injection artifact, and proposes delivery-side fixes.
observations: It is an open issue in the provider-registry channel, authored by claude_code session ch-3mpkjg3r
It explicitly refutes board://item-42e4b0ad and links to same-law board://item-abe0bbbd
It cites 31 ChatGPT messages: 10 byte-identical pairs with identical md5 but different timestamps, plus 11 genuinely distinct messages
It identifies a regular paired-delay structure as the signature of delivery-layer replay rather than model recomposition
It proposes a consumption cursor, an already-actioned/dedupe guard, and a hydration wrapper as fixes
It frames the conclusion as evidence, not a verdict
standouts: The byte-identical pairs follow a regular paired-delay pattern, which is used to rule out ChatGPT re-composition
It shifts blame from ChatGPT amnesia/looping to channel-delivery re-injection
It ties the diagnosis to the same state-handoff family as item-abe0bbbd
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Body is Markdown with labeled EVIDENCE and FIX sections
References other board items via board:// URIs with semantic link kinds
Ends with an explicit evidentiary framing rather than a definitive verdict
concerns: The cc_channels log and md5 verification details are referenced but not included in the file
Named individuals (Tim and the lead) lack surrounding context
The proposed fixes are described at a high level without implementation specifics
notes: This issue uses channel-log forensics to argue that repeated ChatGPT messages are delivery-layer replays, not model looping, and points toward cursor/dedupe/hydration fixes.
questions: What is the cc_channels log and how was the md5-based verification performed?
What specific claim does item-42e4b0ad make that this item refutes?
How should the consumption-cursor, dedupe guard, and hydration wrapper be implemented?
Who are Tim and the lead, and what are their roles in this incident?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—correcting the misattribution of repeats to ChatGPT—matches the content, which presents evidence and a delivery-layer diagnosis.
apparent_themes: Distinguishing channel replay from model recomposition
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to correct a misdiagnosis by demonstrating that observed message duplication is a channel-delivery replay artifact and to steer remediation toward delivery-side cursor/dedupe/hydration changes.
novelty: It uses a regular paired-delay structure across byte-identical message pairs as a forensic signature to distinguish channel replay from model amnesia.
contribution: It contributes a documented, evidence-backed alternative explanation for duplicated messages, a set of proposed delivery-layer fixes, and explicit relationships to related board items.
summary_for_embedding: Open provider-registry noticeboard issue that refutes a ChatGPT-looping claim by presenting channel-log evidence of byte-identical message pairs with regular paired delays, diagnosing a delivery-layer replay, and proposing a consumption cursor, dedupe guard, and hydration wrapper.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to correct the attribution of repeated ChatGPT messages from model amnesia to channel-delivery replay and to guide delivery-layer remediation.
